tweets.text:
I’m inspired by young people putting frontier AI to work in science in new and surprising ways.

We recently met a young astronomer who used AI to analyze massive datasets. He identified 1.5 million new celestial objects in space, and the head of NASA offered him a job and a ride in a fighter jet as a signing bonus! (https://t.co/v68AdOyShw)

To celebrate stand-out students and recent grads like this—and help keep their ideas moving—we’re launching a new program: ChatGPT 26. If you're selected, we'll host you at OpenAI HQ in SF, give you access to our latest tech, and give you a $10K cash grant to keep pushing your ideas forward.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to meet you and hear your story. Apply or nominate someone here: https://t.co/jqm2jPOiM4
tweets.summary_zh: OpenAI 推出了「ChatGPT 26」计划，专门支持用 AI 做出色研究的学生和应届毕业生。入选的人会被邀请到 OpenAI 总部、拿到最新技术权限和 1 万美元资助。他还提到一个年轻天文学家用 AI 分析数据发现了 150 万个新天体，NASA 局长直接给了他一份 offer。

tweets.text:
I have a friend who has a high schooler who is getting rejected from a bunch of colleges.

Apparently, it's much harder to get in to a good college now than before.

But college shouldn't be make or break for what you want to do in life. I hope more kids can just realize that gatekeepers can't stop you if you just persist in building something that people want.
tweets.summary_zh: 朋友家的高中生申请大学被拒了好几所，现在上好大学越来越难了。但大学不应该决定你的人生，只要坚持做出人们需要的东西，没有谁能真正挡住你。

tweets.text:
Sometimes you have to take a moment, step back, and realize how insane this is. 

Codex noticed that I included a line in a prompt that obliquely references a feature that's behind a feature flag elsewhere in the app, and flagged it.

What % of human reviewers would catch this? https://t.co/2vqBMfTlX3
tweets.summary_zh: 被 Codex（OpenAI 的代码审查 AI）震惊了——它竟然能发现代码里一个隐晦地引用了还在内测中的功能，这种细节可能大部分人类审查者都注意不到。

twitter.name: Aaron Levie
twitter.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
Computer use and the ability to write and run code on the fly are the ultimate primitives for agents to be able to take on more and more tasks in knowledge work. 

Most work requires hopping between multiple applications, and working with broad sets of data, in a workflow, and agents will need to be able to traverse these systems to be able to effectively automate any real work in the enterprise. 

Now we will have agents that are the equivalent of having an expert programmer (or any number of them) that can write code or use any API to automate whatever work you’re doing. Agents will have access to either a user’s computer and resources, or their own sandbox to operate in, and be able to pull together the tools necessary to perform the task at hand. This opens up the broadest set of agentic use-cases.

To be sure, there are going to be various hurdles around security, permissions and access controls, identity challenges, and more. 

For instance, should the agent always act on behalf of the user, or should they have their own identity and limited set of access rights? How do you triage security events when historically volume of activity on a system is no longer a reliable signal of a security issue? How do you ensure the agent isn’t going rogue or getting prompt injected to do something risky? All problems that need to get figured out.

Then, there’s also lots of work needed to ensure software is setup to enable to agents to operate with their tools in a headless fashion. This will be an uncomfortable reality for some incumbents, and equally a welcome one for tools that historically have operated seamlessly via APIs, and have business models to support this.

Lots of change coming in the world of work agents, and it’s going to get pretty wild.
tweets.summary_zh: 深度分析了 AI Agent（智能代理）的未来：电脑操控能力加上实时写代码的能力，是让 AI 完成更多知识工作的关键。大部分工作需要在多个应用之间跳来跳去，Agent 必须能跨系统操作才能真正自动化企业工作。当然还有安全、权限、身份验证等一堆问题要解决，但变化已经来了。

twitter.bio_zh: Box CEO，企业 AI 应用的坚定推动者，科技圈知名段子手

tweets.text:
GStack is great for solo devs and for people making projects from scratch. This much is true. 

But what I learned tonight is that the skills transfer to giant codebases with a lot of tech debt, and I hadn't realized that could work until now. 

Try it!

https://t.co/VPvWDzV5c0
tweets.summary_zh: 分享了一个发现：GStack 不只适合从零开始的小项目和个人开发者，对有大量历史技术债务的大型代码库同样好用。推荐大家试试。

tweets.text:
Just created my first PR into the Y Combinator codebase (1.84M lines of code total, this PR was 2400 lines) 

I got the YC internal/bookface codebase to run natively in @conductor_build - and it was hairy since it's a gargantuan codebase that I hadn't worked with in more than a decade

So it turns out GStack can help a ton even with giant production codebases with 14 years of technical debt (I made this repo and made Bookface back in 2013).
tweets.summary_zh: 刚给 Y Combinator 的内部代码库提交了第一个 PR（代码修改请求），这个代码库有 184 万行代码。他用 AI 工具 Conductor 成功跑通了这个他十多年没碰过的老项目，证明 AI 编程工具对大型老旧代码库也很有帮助。

twitter.bio_zh: Y Combinator 现任 CEO，早期投资了 Coinbase、Instacart 等

tweets.text:
Pretty much every PR I review:

0) review <URL> [codex does it's thing and finds issues]
1) is the issue clear? [if not, trash PR]
2) is this the best possible fix? [95% of the time no]
3) continue discussion, consider tradeoffs, usually rewrite PR

Most folks send too localized, small fixes that would end up making the project unmaintainable.
tweets.summary_zh: 分享了自己审查代码 PR 的流程：先用 Codex（AI 代码审查工具）看一遍找问题，然后判断问题是否清晰、修复方案是否最优。大部分人提交的修改都太局部、太小，长期下去会让项目变得难以维护。

tweets.text:
I have loved being on the Helion board; I continue to be extremely excited about a future with abundant energy and Helion in particular.

As Helion and OpenAI start to explore working together at significant scale, it is difficult for me to be on both boards. (I will have a financial interest in Helion so still be recused from negotiations, but from a governance perspective this will make things easier for both companies.)

David and Chris are exceptional founders and I think people will be quite impressed by the company's progress.
tweets.summary_zh: 宣布自己将退出 Helion（一家核聚变能源公司）的董事会。因为 Helion 和 OpenAI 要开始大规模合作，他同时在两边的董事会会有治理上的冲突。他仍然看好 Helion 和丰富能源的未来，也夸了 Helion 的两位创始人很厉害。

twitter.name: Claude
twitter.bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on desktop or download the app.

tweets.text:
Available on Pro and Max. 

Update your desktop app and pair with mobile to try: https://t.co/YhYFTjHZYK
tweets.summary_zh: Claude 电脑操控功能已上线，Pro 和 Max 用户可用，更新桌面端应用并配合手机端即可体验。

tweets.text:
Assign a task from your phone, turn your attention to something else, and come back to finished work on your computer.

Tell Claude once to scan your email every morning or pull a report every Friday, and it handles it from there. https://t.co/HeaBKnPl8D
tweets.summary_zh: 介绍 Claude 的新功能：在手机上给 Claude 布置任务，然后去忙别的，回来时电脑上已经帮你做完了。还可以设定定期任务，比如每天早上扫一遍邮件、每周五拉一份报告，Claude 会自动执行。

tweets.text:
Claude uses your connected apps first: Slack, Calendar, and other integrations.

When there's no connector for the tool you need, it asks for your permission to open the app on your screen directly. https://t.co/19b6sl6irI
tweets.summary_zh: 解释 Claude 的工作方式：优先通过已连接的应用（如 Slack、日历等）来完成任务，如果没有对应的集成工具，会在征得你同意后直接在电脑屏幕上打开对应的应用来操作。

twitter.bio_zh: Anthropic 旗下 AI 助手 Claude 的官方账号

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: Building AI Product Backwards #ai #podcast #podcastclips
podcasts.summary_zh: Data Driven NYC 的一个短片段，聊的是「反向构建 AI 产品」的思路——不是先有技术再找场景，而是从用户问题倒推产品设计。适合产品经理和创业者快速看一下，几分钟就能 get 到核心观点，属于轻量级的思维启发。

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: Andrej Karpathy on Claude, Codex, and OpenClaw
podcasts.summary_zh: 重磅访谈！Karpathy 在 No Priors 上深聊 AI 编程工具，横向对比了 Claude Code、OpenAI Codex 和 OpenClaw，还分享了他对 vibe coding 和 AI 辅助开发未来的看法。如果你在用或者在选 AI 编程工具，这期必看，信息量非常大。

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: Andrej Karpathy Automated His Home
podcasts.summary_zh: Karpathy 聊了聊他用 AI 和自动化改造自己家的经历，算是一个很有趣的个人项目分享。比起硬核技术讨论，这期更轻松好玩，能看到顶级 AI 研究者在生活中怎么折腾技术。适合休闲的时候看，会觉得挺有意思。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: The UI Problem of Chatbots #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh: 讨论一个很多人都有感觉但说不清楚的问题：聊天机器人的 UI 到底哪里不对？为什么纯对话框不是最好的交互方式？对做 AI 产品设计的人特别有启发，短片段节奏快，适合通勤路上听一下。

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: Dreamer: the Agent OS for Everyone — David Singleton
podcasts.summary_zh: Latent Space 请来了前 Stripe CTO David Singleton，聊他的新项目 Dreamer——一个让所有人都能搭建和运行 AI Agent 的「Agent 操作系统」。这期偏深度访谈，适合对 AI Agent 平台和基础设施感兴趣的人认真看，干货不少。

🌐 Make websites accessible for AI agents. Automate tasks online with ease.

You like pytorch? You like micrograd? You love tinygrad! ❤️
The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.
The agent that grows with you
🚀🚀 「大模型」2小时完全从0训练26M的小参数GPT！🌏 Train a 26M-parameter GPT from scratch in just 2h!
